1: package session
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"database/sql"
6: 	"fmt"
7: 	"sort"
8: 	"strings"
9: 
10: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/db"
11: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/db/timestamp"
12: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/errors"
13: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/kms"
14: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/perms"
15: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/types/action"
16: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/types/resource"
17: )
18: 
19: // Clonable provides a cloning interface
20: type Cloneable interface {
21: 	Clone() interface{}
22: }
23: 
24: // Repository is the session database repository
25: type Repository struct {
26: 	reader db.Reader
27: 	writer db.Writer
28: 	kms    *kms.Kms
29: 
30: 	// defaultLimit provides a default for limiting the number of results returned from the repo
31: 	defaultLimit int
32: 
33: 	permissions *perms.UserPermissions
34: }
35: 
36: // RepositoryFactory is a function that creates a Repository.
37: type RepositoryFactory func(opt ...Option) (*Repository, error)
38: 
39: // NewRepository creates a new session Repository. Supports the options: WithLimit
40: // which sets a default limit on results returned by repo operations.
41: func NewRepository(r db.Reader, w db.Writer, kms *kms.Kms, opt ...Option) (*Repository, error) {
42: 	const op = "session.NewRepository"
43: 	if r == nil {
44: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(errors.InvalidParameter, op, "nil reader")
45: 	}
46: 	if w == nil {
47: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(errors.InvalidParameter, op, "nil writer")
48: 	}
49: 	if kms == nil {
50: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(errors.InvalidParameter, op, "nil kms")
51: 	}
52: 	opts := getOpts(opt...)
53: 	if opts.withLimit == 0 {
54: 		// zero signals the boundary defaults should be used.
55: 		opts.withLimit = db.DefaultLimit
56: 	}
57: 
58: 	if opts.withPermissions != nil {
59: 		for _, p := range opts.withPermissions.Permissions {
60: 			if p.Resource != resource.Session {
61: 				return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(errors.InvalidParameter, op, "permission for incorrect resource")
62: 			}
63: 		}
64: 	}
65: 
66: 	return &Repository{
67: 		reader:       r,
68: 		writer:       w,
69: 		kms:          kms,
70: 		defaultLimit: opts.withLimit,
71: 		permissions:  opts.withPermissions,
72: 	}, nil
73: }
74: 
75: func (r *Repository) listPermissionWhereClauses() ([]string, []interface{}) {
76: 	var where []string
77: 	var args []interface{}
78: 
79: 	if r.permissions == nil {
80: 		return where, args
81: 	}
82: 
83: 	inClauseCnt := 0
84: 	for _, p := range r.permissions.Permissions {
85: 		if p.Action != action.List {
86: 			continue
87: 		}
88: 
89: 		inClauseCnt++
90: 
91: 		var clauses []string
92: 		clauses = append(clauses, fmt.Sprintf("project_id = @project_id_%d", inClauseCnt))
93: 		args = append(args, sql.Named(fmt.Sprintf("project_id_%d", inClauseCnt), p.ScopeId))
94: 
95: 		if len(p.ResourceIds) > 0 {
96: 			clauses = append(clauses, fmt.Sprintf("public_id = any(@public_id_%d)", inClauseCnt))
97: 			args = append(args, sql.Named(fmt.Sprintf("public_id_%d", inClauseCnt), "{"+strings.Join(p.ResourceIds, ",")+"}"))
98: 		}
99: 
100: 		if p.OnlySelf {
101: 			inClauseCnt++
102: 			clauses = append(clauses, fmt.Sprintf("user_id = @user_id_%d", inClauseCnt))
103: 			args = append(args, sql.Named(fmt.Sprintf("user_id_%d", inClauseCnt), r.permissions.UserId))
104: 		}
105: 
106: 		where = append(where, fmt.Sprintf("(%s)", strings.Join(clauses, " and ")))
107: 	}
108: 	return where, args
109: }
110: 
111: // list will return a listing of resources and honor the WithLimit option or the
112: // repo defaultLimit.  Supports WithOrder option.
113: func (r *Repository) list(ctx context.Context, resources interface{}, where string, args []interface{}, opt ...Option) error {
114: 	const op = "session.(Repository).list"
115: 	opts := getOpts(opt...)
116: 	limit := r.defaultLimit
117: 	var dbOpts []db.Option
118: 	if opts.withLimit != 0 {
119: 		// non-zero signals an override of the default limit for the repo.
120: 		limit = opts.withLimit
121: 	}
122: 	dbOpts = append(dbOpts, db.WithLimit(limit))
123: 	switch opts.withOrderByCreateTime {
124: 	case db.AscendingOrderBy:
125: 		dbOpts = append(dbOpts, db.WithOrder("create_time asc"))
126: 	case db.DescendingOrderBy:
127: 		dbOpts = append(dbOpts, db.WithOrder("create_time"))
128: 	}
129: 	if err := r.reader.SearchWhere(ctx, resources, where, args, dbOpts...); err != nil {
130: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
131: 	}
132: 	return nil
133: }
134: 
135: func (r *Repository) convertToSessions(ctx context.Context, sessionList []*sessionListView, opt ...Option) ([]*Session, error) {
136: 	const op = "session.(Repository).convertToSessions"
137: 	opts := getOpts(opt...)
138: 
139: 	if len(sessionList) == 0 {
140: 		return nil, nil
141: 	}
142: 	sessions := []*Session{}
143: 	// deduplication map of states by end_time
144: 	states := map[*timestamp.Timestamp]*State{}
145: 	var prevSessionId string
146: 	var workingSession *Session
147: 	for _, sv := range sessionList {
148: 		if sv.PublicId != prevSessionId {
149: 			if prevSessionId != "" {
150: 				for _, s := range states {
151: 					workingSession.States = append(workingSession.States, s)

153: 				states = map[*timestamp.Timestamp]*State{}
154: 				sort.Slice(workingSession.States, func(i, j int) bool {
155: 					return workingSession.States[i].StartTime.GetTimestamp().AsTime().After(workingSession.States[j].StartTime.GetTimestamp().AsTime())
156: 				})
157: 				sessions = append(sessions, workingSession)
158: 			}
159: 			prevSessionId = sv.PublicId
160: 			workingSession = &Session{
161: 				PublicId:          sv.PublicId,

163: 				HostId:            sv.HostId,
164: 				TargetId:          sv.TargetId,
165: 				HostSetId:         sv.HostSetId,
166: 				AuthTokenId:       sv.AuthTokenId,
167: 				ProjectId:         sv.ProjectId,
168: 				Certificate:       sv.Certificate,
169: 				ExpirationTime:    sv.ExpirationTime,
170: 				CtTofuToken:       sv.CtTofuToken,
171: 				TofuToken:         sv.TofuToken, // will always be nil since it's not stored in the database.
172: 				TerminationReason: sv.TerminationReason,
173: 				CreateTime:        sv.CreateTime,
174: 				UpdateTime:        sv.UpdateTime,
175: 				Version:           sv.Version,
176: 				Endpoint:          sv.Endpoint,
177: 				ConnectionLimit:   sv.ConnectionLimit,
178: 				KeyId:             sv.KeyId,
179: 			}
180: 			if opts.withListingConvert {
181: 				workingSession.CtTofuToken = nil // CtTofuToken should not returned in lists
182: 				workingSession.TofuToken = nil   // TofuToken should not returned in lists
183: 				workingSession.KeyId = ""        // KeyId should not be returned in lists
184: 			} else {

196: 			}

state.output:
<path>/private/var/folders/9f/txt0j2rs2wx3lqx9vfj9x3240000gn/T/BenchmarkRenamegpt-oss-20b-mediumboundary_eb61ac63raw767426141/003/boundary/internal/server/repository.go</path>

<content>
1: package server
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"reflect"
5: 	"time"
6: 
7: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/db"
8: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/errors"
9: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/kms"
10: )
11: 
12: const (
13: 	// DefaultLiveness is the setting that controls the server "liveness" time,
14: 	// or the maximum allowable time that a worker can't send a status update to
15: 	// the controller for. After this, the server is considered dead, and it will
16: 	// be taken out of the rotation for allowable workers for connections, and
17: 	// connections will possibly start to be terminated and marked as closed
18: 	// depending on the grace period setting (see
19: 	// base.Server.StatusGracePeriodDuration). This value serves as the default
20: 	// and minimum allowable setting for the grace period.
21: 	DefaultLiveness = 15 * time.Second
22: )
23: 
24: // Repository is the server database repository
25: type Repository struct {
26: 	reader db.Reader
27: 	writer db.Writer
28: 	kms    *kms.Kms
29: 	// defaultLimit provides a default for limiting the number of results returned from the repo
30: 	defaultLimit int
31: }
32: 
33: // NewRepository creates a new server Repository. Supports the options: WithLimit
34: // which sets a default limit on results returned by repo operations.
35: func NewRepository(r db.Reader, w db.Writer, kms *kms.Kms, opt ...Option) (*Repository, error) {
36: 	const op = "server.NewRepository"
37: 	if r == nil {
38: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(errors.InvalidParameter, op, "nil reader")
39: 	}
40: 	if w == nil {
41: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(errors.InvalidParameter, op, "nil writer")
42: 	}
43: 	if kms == nil {
44: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(errors.InvalidParameter, op, "nil kms")
45: 	}
46: 
47: 	opts := GetOpts(opt...)
48: 	if opts.withLimit == 0 {
49: 		// zero signals the boundary defaults should be used.
50: 		opts.withLimit = db.DefaultLimit
51: 	}
52: 	return &Repository{
53: 		reader:       r,
54: 		writer:       w,
55: 		kms:          kms,
56: 		defaultLimit: opts.withLimit,
57: 	}, nil
58: }
59: 
60: func isNil(i interface{}) bool {
61: 	if i == nil {
62: 		return true
63: 	}
64: 	switch reflect.TypeOf(i).Kind() {
65: 	case reflect.Ptr, reflect.Map, reflect.Array, reflect.Chan, reflect.Slice:
66: 		return reflect.ValueOf(i).IsNil()
67: 	}
68: 	return false
69: }
